diffusion_technique.sky_intro_1: Imagine your mind as a wide open sky.
diffusion_technique.sky_intro_2: Thoughts are like clouds passing through.
diffusion_technique.sky_intro_3: Some are light and fluffy. Some are dark and stormy.
diffusion_technique.sky_intro_5: Your job is simply to watch them pass.
diffusion_technique.sky_question: What thought is on your mind right now?
diffusion_technique.sky_hint: Write one thought that has been bothering you recently.
diffusion_technique.reflection_hint: How did it feel watching the thought instead of holding onto it?
diffusion_technique.sell_intro_1: Sometimes our mind tries to sell us thoughts that feel very convincing.
diffusion_technique.sell_intro_2: In this exercise you will treat your thought like a product someone is trying to sell you.
diffusion_technique.sell_intro_3: Let's see how valuable it really is.
diffusion_technique.sell_cost_question: How much would this thought cost?
diffusion_technique.sell_cost_hint: If someone tried to sell you this thought, how valuable would it actually be?
diffusion_technique.sell_result_low_desc: Looks like this thought may not be very valuable.
Sometimes our mind tries to sell us things we don't need to buy.
diffusion_technique.sell_result_mid_desc: This thought has some pull, but it's not the whole story.
Noticing it is the first step toward gaining perspective.
diffusion_technique.sell_result_high_des: This thought feels convincing right now.
Noticing it is the first step toward gaining distance from it.
diffusion_technique.name_intro_1: Our mind often repeats the same thoughts again and again.
diffusion_technique.name_intro_2: Instead of fighting them, we can simply name the story our mind is telling.
diffusion_technique.name_intro_3: This helps us notice the thought instead of getting caught in it.
diffusion_technique.name_step4_desc1: Next time this thought appears, try saying:
diffusion_technique.name_step4_phrase_pr: I'm noticing my mind telling the
diffusion_technique.name_step4_desc2: This small shift helps you step back from the thought.
diffusion_technique.conclusion_sky_desc1: Thoughts are like clouds in the sky.
diffusion_technique.conclusion_sky_desc2: They appear, move across our mind, and eventually pass.
diffusion_technique.conclusion_sky_desc3: You practiced watching the thought instead of holding onto it.
diffusion_technique.conclusion_sky_desc4: The more you practice this, the easier it becomes to let thoughts come and go.
diffusion_technique.conclusion_sell_desc: Not every thought that appears in our mind deserves our attention.
By evaluating its value, you practiced stepping back and questioning the thought instead of automatically believing it.
Sometimes thoughts sound convincing — but they aren't always worth buying.
diffusion_technique.conclusion_name_desc: Our minds often repeat the same stories again and again.
By giving the thought a name, you practiced recognizing the story instead of getting caught in it.
Next time it appears, you can simply notice:
diffusion_technique.intro_desc1: Sometimes our thoughts feel overwhelming because we treat them as facts.
diffusion_technique.intro_desc2: Diffusion techniques help you step back and observe thoughts instead of getting stuck in them.
diffusion_technique.intro_desc3: In this activity you will try simple exercises that help your mind create space from unhelpful thoughts.
diffusion_technique.choose_desc: Each exercise helps you look at your thoughts from a new perspective. Pick one and follow the guided steps.
diffusion_technique.card_sky_desc: Imagine your thoughts drifting across the sky like clouds. Watch them appear and pass by without holding onto them.
diffusion_technique.card_sell_desc: Treat your thought like something being sold to you. How valuable is it really?
diffusion_technique.card_name_desc: When thoughts repeat again and again, they become stories. Naming the story helps create distance from it.
